{"step_by_step_thinking": "The patient is a 1-month-old child with conjugated bilirubinemia and intrahepatic cholestasis. On liver biopsy, red-colored granules were seen inside the hepatocytes. This finding is characteristic of alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, which is known to cause PAS-positive granules in the hepatocytes. Other conditions like congenital hepatic fibrosis, Wilson's disease, and hemochromatosis do not typically present with this specific histological finding. Therefore, the probable diagnosis is alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency.", "answer_choice": "C"}
